_notes: 1. The ground surface is set at +28.79 m above sea level (NN) in front of house 73 (reference point according to the development plan). This results in the building zero level of +28.97 m above sea level (NN) = upper edge of the finished floor of the ground floor (§ 2 paragraph 3 HBauO).

2. The start of construction must be notified to the building supervisory authority at least one week in advance (§ 72a paragraph 4 HBauO). Please use the form available on the online service "Notice of start, demolition and resumption of construction" on the website gateway.hamburg.de.

3. The builder must notify the building supervisory authority of the intended occupancy at least two weeks in advance. This does not apply to the demolition of facilities and the construction of non-structural advertising facilities (§ 77 paragraph 2 HBauO).

4. The outdoor areas must be created in accordance with § 2 of the development plan LM 20 and § 9 paragraph 1 HBauO until the final completion of the building. In case of failure, equivalent replacements must be planted. (see also points 6 to 10 in the decision)
6. Trees must be protected during construction with a fixed tree protection fence, securely anchored in the ground, at a distance of 4 m from the tree trunk, or similar measures.

7. Tree care measures must be carried out by a professional tree care company in accordance with the tree care standards (ZTV - current edition) while maintaining the natural growth form of the trees.
8. Excavations in the root area must be carried out using root-friendly methods (by hand or using a suction excavator). Damaged roots must be cut cleanly above the injury.

9. Due to the determined value of the tree stock according to the tree evaluation (Appendix 4/8), the nature conservation compensation requirement is set at 4 trees or € 4,000. This compensation will be partly in the form of planting 3 medium-crowned deciduous trees and a payment of € 1,000, as a new planting of 4 medium-crowned deciduous trees cannot be sustainably realized on the property.
10. The compensation plantings must be carried out in accordance with the planting quality specified in the tree inventory and evaluation.
11. A maintenance contract for the compensation plantings must be established with a qualified gardening company to ensure successful establishment of the plants for at least 2 years.
12. The compensation planting must be reported to the relevant authority after completion of the exterior facilities.
13. An offset payment of € 1,000 must be made for financing nature conservation and landscape maintenance measures.

building_regulations_and_requirements.6_compensation_for_felling_measuresrepla: Due to the determined value of the tree stock according to the tree evaluation (Appendix 20/30), the nature conservation replacement requirement is set at 12 trees or €12,000. building_regulations_and_requirements.4_if_irregularities_in_the_subsoil_disco: 040/428 40-2300, must be informed (§ 1 Abs. 1 Hamburgisches Bodenschutzgesetz).
_notes: 1. The building permit expires if construction has not begun within three years of issuance or if construction is interrupted for more than one year. It can be extended on written application for up to one year each time.
2. The approval does not replace the need to obtain other public law decisions.

1. On the mentioned property, there is an area no. 7620-101/00 (former gas station) classified as "completed (was not AvF)" registered in the soil condition register of the Authority for Environment and Energy (BUE). During construction measures and earthworks, additional costs for the disposal of excavated soil or construction pit water may arise.

2. The property is located in a project area of the Authority for Environment and Energy (BUE) (GasBW-000), which includes an area of the Elbmarsch, where organic soft layers (clay, mud, and peat) are present in the subsoil. Due to decomposition processes in these soils, soil gases (methane [CH4] and carbon dioxide [CO2]) can naturally form. These gases can rise to near-surface soil layers, accumulate especially under sealed/built-up areas, and potentially penetrate buildings. However, according to current knowledge, these soft layers are not present in relevant thicknesses (> 2m) for the formation of soil gases in the area of the property. If soft layers with a thickness > 2m are encountered or known in the area of the construction project, structural safety measures to prevent gas accumulations and gas entries into the building must be provided, such as a gravel filter layer, gas-tight pipe penetrations, no enclosed spaces below the floor (§ 16 HBauO).

3. Topsoil must be preserved in a usable condition and protected from destruction or waste (§ 202 Baugesetzbuch). Excavated material, including topsoil, must be reused or recycled according to the applicable waste and soil protection regulations of the Federal Republic and the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg in accordance with § 6 Kreislaufwirtschaftsgesetz (KrWG). If reuse or recycling is not feasible according to legal requirements, the excavated material must be disposed of. For the reuse of excavated material suitable for creating a rootable soil layer or for placing in rootable soil layers due to its properties (humus content, absence of contaminants, structure), the requirements of § 12 Bundes-Bodenschutz- und Altlastenverordnung (BBodSchV) must be observed.
5. The start of construction must be reported to the building supervisory authority at least one week in advance (§ 72a Abs. 4 HBauO).
6. The intended use of the building must be reported to the building supervisory authority at least two weeks in advance (§ 77 Abs. 2 HBauO).

decision_basis.regulations: WR, max. II, building boundary
_notes: Baunutzungsverordnung from June 26, 1962

subjects: felling the obstructive tree No. 1 (Spruce) and the obstructive hedges Nos. H4 and H5 (total of 39 meters) (see attachment 6 / 16).

conditions: Planting of one small to medium-crowned tree; planting in minimum quality 3 x v STU 18-20 cm using suitable native species. Each tree must have a rootable vegetation area of at least 12 square meters.

Planting of hedges using suitable native species, minimum length of 39 meters, with at least 3 plants per meter in minimum quality 3 x v m.B. 100-125 cm. A vegetation strip with a minimum width of 1 meter must be maintained.

The planting measures must be executed by a qualified gardening company in consultation with the landscape architect. They must be carried out in the planting season after construction completion, no later than April 30, and must be permanently maintained. In case of failure, equivalent replacements must be planted by April 30, 2025.
After fulfilling the requirements, the district office must be notified in writing with a planting plan and proof of the planting obligation.
To ensure the growth of replacement measures, a qualified gardening company must provide at least two years of development maintenance.

subjects: exceptions, the Authority for Environment, Climate, Energy, and Agriculture, N3 Species and Biotope Protection, must be contacted.

property_information.project_description: New construction of two 2-story single houses, consisting of 3 buildings each with 2 residential units and shared basement

_notes: 1. The building permit expires if construction has not begun within three years of issuance or if construction is interrupted for more than one year. It can be extended on written application for up to one year each time.
2. The approval does not replace the need to obtain other public law decisions.

1. Tree protection requirements for the entire construction period, including securing the crown and root areas of Oaks Nr. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 9, and 10 according to the tree stock plan (attachment 4/4) and tree expert opinion (attachment 4/8). Protection methods include fixed tree protection fences and root protection curtains.
2. Tree protection measures and ecological supervision must be presented to the mentioned office before the start of construction for approval.

1. Due to the tree removal, a replacement of 4 trees or €4,000 is required. Given the site's constraints, the replacement includes planting 3 medium-crowned native trees and a compensatory payment of €1,000.

2. The replacement trees must be planted by a qualified gardening company by April 30, 2022, with a minimum trunk circumference of 18-20 cm. The planted trees must be maintained for at least two years to ensure growth.
3. The compensatory payment of €1,000 must be used for nature conservation and landscape management measures.
